methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
Observable Space, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Dan Roelker and Rick Hedrick, has raised $20M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on two applicable methods triangulated against a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, anchoring to the $20M raised proxy to yield $40M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), Sift ($274M, Series B), and Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B)—produces a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (median 53.2x across the five tickers) is not applicable absent disclosed revenue figures.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $34M to $460M, with a median of $220M. This wide dispersion reflects stage and maturity variance within the peer set—the presence of later-stage Series B comparables inflates the median estimate. Observable Space's valuation likely clusters toward the lower end absent material revenue traction or material follow-on funding disclosure. Key uncertainties include revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and whether near-term funding is imminent at a known valuation.
